q: The air hostess told me that I was not allowed to sit in business class. (Change the sentence into the direct speech.) 2024
a: The air hostess said to me, "You are not allowed to sit in business class."

q: Kyaw Kyaw said, "Mi Mi, are you the one who sent me this email yesterday?" (Change the sentence into the reported speech.) 2024
a: Kyaw Kyaw asked Mi Mi if she was the one who had sent him that email the day before.

q: Zaw Zaw asked Mya Mya why she had not come to their Soon-kyway the day before. (Change the sentence into the direct speech.) 2024
a: Zaw Zaw said to /asked Mya Mya, "Why didn't you come to our Soon-Kyway yesterday?" (OR) Zaw Zaw said to/asked , "(Mya Mya), why didn't you come to our Soon-Kyway yesterday, (Mya Mya)?"

q: Ma Ma said to her sister, "Will you come and sit on this sofa if you are free?" (Change the sentence into 'the reported speech.') 2024
a: Ma Ma asked her sister if she would come and sit on that sofa if she was free.

q: Patricia asked me, "Did you visit the zoo last month?" (Change the sentence into the reported speech.) 2024
a: Patricia asked me if I had visited the zoo the previous month.

q: He said to me, "When I reach the airport, I'll call you." (Change the sentence into the reported speech.) 2025
a: He told me that when he reached the airport, he would call me.

q: Ko Ko said to me, "Did you throw away the old magazines on this table yesterday?" (Change the sentence into the reported speech.) 2025
a: Ko Ko asked me if I had thrown away the old magazines on that table the day before.

q: "What shall I do if you don't accompany me to the concert tonight?" Thazin said to her friend. (Change the sentence into 'the reported speech.') 2025
a: Thazin asked her friend what she should do if she did not accompany her to the concert that night.

q: She said to Hla Hla, "I am meeting you at the cafe tomorrow." (Change the sentence into the reported speech.) 2025
a: She told Hla Hla that she was meeting her at the café the next day.

q: Hnin Hnin said to me, "What were you doing when I phoned you this morning?" (Change the sentence into the reported speech.) 2025
a: Hnin Hnin asked me what I had been doing when she had phoned me that morning.

q: Sandar asked him whether he could help her with her homework. (Change the sentence into the direct speech.) 2025
a: Sandar asked, "Can you help me with my homework?"

q: The boy said to his brother, "You don't have to stay in here with me, if it bothers you". (Change the sentence into the reported speech.) 2025
a: The boy told his brother that he didn't have to stay in there with him if it bothered him.

q: Anna said to me, "I might visit you next week if I'm not too busy." (Change the sentence into the reported speech.) 2025
a: Anna said that she might visit me the following week if she wasn't too busy.

q: Mother said to Mu Mu. "Go to bed immediately and don't get up until you are called". (Change the sentence into the reported speech.) 2025
a: Mother told Mu Mu to go to bed immediately and not to get up until she was called.

q: His brother said to me, "I worked as a waiter before becoming a chef." (Change the sentence into the reported speech.) 2026
a: His brother told me that he had worked as a waiter before becoming a chef.

q: The teacher asked us whether we had finished our project yet. (Change the sentence into direct speech.) 2026
a: The teacher said to us, "Have you finished your project yet?"

q: The traffic officer said to the motorcyclist, "Why don't you wear the helmet?" (Change the sentence into the reported speech.) 2026
a: The traffic officer asked the motorcyclist why he didn't wear the helmet.

q: My friends said tome, "We visited the museum last Saturday." (Change the sentence into the reported speech.) 2026
a: My friends told me that they had visited the museum the previous Saturday.
